seed: clean process with a grounded subject; G2 (one real note, tempting fabrications)
passage: In photosynthesis, a plant's leaves absorb sunlight. Chlorophyll captures the light energy and uses it to split water and fix carbon dioxide from the air into glucose, releasing oxygen as a by-product. The glucose stores chemical energy the plant uses to grow. The only note that exists in the vault is the one on photosynthesis itself.
